Next up: Batch #02.

From farm to table

May 12 · 6PM · Akasha House, Poble Nou

Elias grows veggies at Viriditas, a regenerative farm 40 minutes from Barcelona. On May 12, he's bringing a crate of whatever's in season that week, and we're cooking it together at Akasha House.

02 · How it works

Surprisingly little to explain.

You book, you show up, you cook with a crew and a chef, and you leave with the week's food sorted.

Pick your seat — solo, pair, or table of four.

“Book.”

We'll send the address and what to bring (usually just your chef's knife).

“Show up.”

A couple of hours with a crew and a chef you haven't met.

“Cook.”

Walk out with 5 meals. That's it.

“Take home.”

04 · PAST BATCHES

From the last batch.

03 · The story

Why Let's Batch.

This spun off from managing European funds for food-literacy activities in Barcelona.

We spoke with 1,500 people. The pattern was clear: it's not that people don't know what sustainable food is — they don't know how to integrate it into their life.

There's no better way to learn that than meal-prepping together. Twelve people, one kitchen, one Sunday a month. That's Let's Batch.
